message:
fix(resource-tree): prevent cross-cluster cache leak on switch and offline (#6082)

Two related bugs in ClusterResourceTree's per-cluster data cache caused
nodes/pods/etc. from cluster A to appear under cluster B (issue #6051).

1. Race on cluster switch: the caching effect fired whenever any of the
   ~18 useCached* hook return values updated. When the user switched
   from A to B before all of A's in-flight responses resolved, those
   hooks briefly kept returning A's tagged data while selectedCluster
   was already 'B', so A's data was written into the cache under key B.
   Fix: every hook already tags its items with .cluster, so bail out of
   the cache write if any top-level dataset (nodes/pods/deployments/
   services) carries a cluster tag that doesn't match the currently
   captured selectedCluster — wait for a clean render where all hooks
   agree.

2. No invalidation on offline transition: when a cluster became
   unhealthy, its cache entry was never evicted, so stale data captured
   while the cluster was reachable kept rendering.
   Fix: new effect watches `clusters` and drops cache entries for any
   cluster whose healthy flag goes false.

Also captures selectedCluster into a local const at effect run-time so
the cache key is the value the data was fetched for, not whatever the
closure happens to resolve to later.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
